title: Advanced Dynamics and Temporal Cascades
text: Canonical Tocky Manifold constructed cells assigned precise continuous coordinate (Tocky Time), next crucial step determining genes significantly alter expression along developmental trajectory. vignette demonstrates use RunTradeSeqTocky fit Generalized Additive Models (GAMs) PlotTockyHeatmap identify transcriptional cascades.

title: Advanced Dynamics and Temporal Cascades
text: demonstrate power CanonicalTockySeq tradeSeq, simulate dataset true underlying biological structure: * 100 genes simulated peak sequentially along developmental trajectory. * simulate biological knockout (KO) cells 50% reduction expressing ‘Late’ stage genes compared Wild-Type (WT).
code: set.seed(2026) n_genes <- 500 n_cells <- 300  # 1. Assign true developmental time and groups to cells true_time <- runif(n_cells, 0, pi/2) cell_groups <- sample(c("WT", "KO"), n_cells, replace = TRUE) names(cell_groups) <- paste0("Cell_", 1:n_cells)  # 2. Engineer Continuous Temporal Cascades for Top 100 Genes # We use a Gaussian function so each gene peaks sequentially along the trajectory gene_peaks <- seq(0, pi/2, length.out = 100) X_signal <- matrix(0, nrow = 100, ncol = n_cells) for(i in 1:100) {   X_signal[i, ] <- exp( - (true_time - gene_peaks[i])^2 / 0.1) * 20 }  # Add 400 random noise genes X_noise <- matrix(runif(400 * n_cells, 0, 2), nrow = 400, ncol = n_cells) X_base <- rbind(X_signal, X_noise)  # Simulate a biological knockout effect: KO cells fail to fully upregulate 'Late' genes ko_idx <- which(cell_groups == "KO") X_base[70:100, ko_idx] <- X_base[70:100, ko_idx] * 0.5  # Format as non-negative counts for tradeSeq X <- round(X_base + matrix(rnorm(n_genes * n_cells, sd = 1), nrow = n_genes)) X[X < 0] <- 0 colnames(X) <- names(cell_groups) rownames(X) <- paste0("Gene_", 1:n_genes)  # 3. Create Gene Constraints (Z) matching the biological landmarks # Blue (New) peaks at t=0, BlueRed (Persistent) at t=pi/4, Red (Arrested) at t=pi/2 all_gene_peaks <- c(gene_peaks, runif(400, 0, pi/2)) Z <- matrix(0, nrow = n_genes, ncol = 3) colnames(Z) <- c("Blue", "BlueRed", "Red") rownames(Z) <- rownames(X)  Z[, "Blue"]    <- exp( - (all_gene_peaks - 0)^2 / 0.2 ) Z[, "BlueRed"] <- exp( - (all_gene_peaks - pi/4)^2 / 0.2 ) Z[, "Red"]     <- exp( - (all_gene_peaks - pi/2)^2 / 0.2 )  # 4. Reconstruct Manifold tocky_res <- CanonicalTockySeq(X = X, Z = Z) ## Normalization and projection completed...  ## Performing fast partial SVD (irlba) for top 3 components... ## SVD completed... gradient_res <- GradientTockySeq(   res = tocky_res,    B = tocky_res$biplot["Blue",],    BR = tocky_res$biplot["BlueRed",],    R = tocky_res$biplot["Red",] )

title: Advanced Dynamics and Temporal Cascades
text: Fitting GAMs tens thousands genes computationally intensive. SelectTockyGenes function provides rapid linear spline regression filter isolate dynamically varying genes along Tocky Time axis.
code: # Select the top 100 most dynamic genes for a highly impressive cascade dynamic_genes <- SelectTockyGenes(   expression_data = X,    gradient_res = gradient_res,    top_n = 100,    min_expr = 0.05 ) ##   Initial screen: 500 genes passed expression threshold (5.0%). ##   Scoring dynamics for 500 genes... ##   Selected top 100 genes.

title: Advanced Dynamics and Temporal Cascades
text: RunTradeSeqTocky wrapper interfaces directly tradeSeq package fit GAMs. evaluates expression 0-90 degree Tocky Time. providing named vector two conditions (WT KO), tradeSeq test genes significantly different dynamic trajectories two groups.
code: # Fit GAMs and perform statistical testing between WT and KO tradeSeq_res <- RunTradeSeqTocky(   object = X,   gradient_res = gradient_res,   group_by = cell_groups,   genes = dynamic_genes,   n_knots = 5,   n_cores = 1 ) ## Running tradeSeq on 100 genes across 300 cells (2 groups)... ## Testing for differential dynamics (Condition Test)... # View the differential dynamics results (p-values and FDR) head(tradeSeq_res$results) ##          waldStat df       pvalue         padj ## Gene_71  97.80592  5 0.000000e+00 0.000000e+00 ## Gene_73  91.25394  5 0.000000e+00 0.000000e+00 ## Gene_72  99.79666  5 0.000000e+00 0.000000e+00 ## Gene_70 120.21078  5 0.000000e+00 0.000000e+00 ## Gene_74  83.73076  5 1.110223e-16 2.220446e-15 ## Gene_76  79.41389  5 1.110223e-15 1.850372e-14

title: Advanced Dynamics and Temporal Cascades
text: significant genes identified, plotting developmental order reveals sequential transcriptional logic system. PlotTockyHeatmap function supports ordering genes peak activation timing (“peak”) via standard hierarchical clustering (“cluster”). default, applies Z-score row scaling highlight expression shifts.
code: # Plot the expression cascade, ordered by the peak Tocky Time PlotTockyHeatmap(   object = X,   gradient_res = gradient_res,   genes = dynamic_genes,   ordering_method = "peak",   n_bins = 50,   span = 0.5 ) ## Binning 300 cells into 50 angular intervals... ## Smoothing binned trajectories...

title: Advanced Dynamics and Temporal Cascades
text: heatmap visualizes transcriptional cascade along Canonical Tocky Manifold. allows identification specific temporal windows, identifying immediate-early genes initiate upon TCR signalling versus late-stage markers associated antigen-experienced states. X-axis (Tocky Time): Represents progression cells ‘New’ state ‘Arrested’ state, decoupled absolute signaling intensity. Y-axis (Genes): Displays top 100 dynamic genes, ordered sequentially peak expression timing.

title: Getting Started with CanonicalTockySeq
text: CanonicalTockySeq utilizes supervised Canonical Redundancy Analysis (RDA) construct transcriptomic manifold conical geometry. anchoring single-cell expression data known developmental landmarks, allows researchers accurately trace continuous vivo trajectories. vignette demonstrates core workflow: simulating structured transcriptomic manifold, generating canonical space, projecting cells via piecewise Spherical Linear Interpolation (SLERP), visualizing resulting trajectory.

title: Getting Started with CanonicalTockySeq
text: demonstrate workflow, generate synthetic single-cell dataset true underlying biological structure. engineer continuous temporal cascades using Gaussian functions genes peak sequentially along developmental trajectory, mirroring real biological differentiation. CanonicalTockySeq function requires two inputs: X: numeric matrix gene expression (Genes x Cells). Z: numeric matrix explanatory variables (Genes x Signatures), representing Tocky-specific landmark signatures.
code: set.seed(2026)  n_genes <- 500 n_cells <- 300  # 1. Assign true developmental time to cells true_time <- runif(n_cells, 0, pi/2) cell_names <- paste0("Cell_", 1:n_cells)  # 2. Engineer Continuous Temporal Cascades for Top 100 Genes # We use a Gaussian function so each gene peaks sequentially along the trajectory gene_peaks <- seq(0, pi/2, length.out = 100) X_signal <- matrix(0, nrow = 100, ncol = n_cells) for(i in 1:100) {   X_signal[i, ] <- exp( - (true_time - gene_peaks[i])^2 / 0.1) * 20 }  # Add 400 random noise genes to mimic background transcription X_noise <- matrix(runif(400 * n_cells, 0, 2), nrow = 400, ncol = n_cells) X_base <- rbind(X_signal, X_noise)  # Add statistical noise and format as non-negative counts X <- round(X_base + matrix(rnorm(n_genes * n_cells, sd = 1), nrow = n_genes)) X[X < 0] <- 0 colnames(X) <- cell_names rownames(X) <- paste0("Gene_", 1:n_genes)  # 3. Create Gene Constraints (Z) matching the biological landmarks # Blue (New) peaks at t=0, BlueRed (Persistent) at t=pi/4, Red (Arrested) at t=pi/2 all_gene_peaks <- c(gene_peaks, runif(400, 0, pi/2)) Z <- matrix(0, nrow = n_genes, ncol = 3) colnames(Z) <- c("Blue", "BlueRed", "Red") rownames(Z) <- rownames(X)  Z[, "Blue"]    <- exp( - (all_gene_peaks - 0)^2 / 0.2 ) Z[, "BlueRed"] <- exp( - (all_gene_peaks - pi/4)^2 / 0.2 ) Z[, "Red"]     <- exp( - (all_gene_peaks - pi/2)^2 / 0.2 )

title: Getting Started with CanonicalTockySeq
text: Now apply RDA architecture structured data, treating genes “sites” cells “species” constrained Tocky stages.
code: # Perform the RDA procedure tocky_res <- CanonicalTockySeq(X = X, Z = Z) ## Normalization and projection completed...  ## Performing fast partial SVD (irlba) for top 3 components... ## SVD completed... # The result contains cell_scores and biplot vectors representing the constraints head(tocky_res$cell_scores) ##            Axis1      Axis2       Axis3 ## Cell_1 -4.899648 -0.4118435  0.07252784 ## Cell_2 -3.354054 -5.2585889 -0.07559201 ## Cell_3  7.722975  1.8536392  0.03377961 ## Cell_4  3.640430 -2.8359025  0.09108674 ## Cell_5 -3.306396 -5.1053278 -0.07724761 ## Cell_6  7.983498  2.5867261 -0.08206352

title: Getting Started with CanonicalTockySeq
text: resolve temporal gradient, project cells onto piecewise SLERP manifold. define landmarks based biplot vectors extracted directly RDA.
code: # Extract landmark vectors from the RDA biplot landmark_B  <- tocky_res$biplot["Blue", ] landmark_BR <- tocky_res$biplot["BlueRed", ] landmark_R  <- tocky_res$biplot["Red", ]  # Calculate Tocky Time (Angle) and Tocky Intensity (Radial norm) gradient_res <- GradientTockySeq(   res = tocky_res,    B = landmark_B,    BR = landmark_BR,    R = landmark_R,    filter_negative = TRUE )  head(gradient_res) ##            angle intensity norm_intensity similarity ## Cell_1 62.030093  4.917462      0.7503280   4.914655 ## Cell_2 47.865077  6.237640      1.0831980   6.237638 ## Cell_3  3.211185  7.942384      0.8115684   7.941769 ## Cell_4 29.200362  4.615557      0.6575578   4.612111 ## Cell_5 47.965652  6.082976      1.0569795   6.082976 ## Cell_6  0.647799  8.392504      0.8855437   8.392467

title: Getting Started with CanonicalTockySeq
text: can now visualize constrained ordination space. Notice simulated temporal cascades naturally form sweeping, continuous biological trajectory. plotCanonicalTocky function displays axes alongside continuous gradient legend Tocky Time.
code: plotCanonicalTocky(tocky_res, gradient_res, alpha_level = 0.5)

title: Getting Started with CanonicalTockySeq
text: continuous temporal gradient established along arched manifold, can rapidly filter genes vary significantly along Tocky Time axis.
code: # Filter for top dynamic genes dynamic_genes <- SelectTockyGenes(   expression_data = X,    gradient_res = gradient_res,    top_n = 5 ) ##   Initial screen: 500 genes passed expression threshold (5.0%). ##   Scoring dynamics for 500 genes... ##   Selected top 5 genes. # Plot the expression dynamics of a top gene using LOESS smoothing plotGeneDynamics(   expression_data = X,    gradient_res = gradient_res,    gene = dynamic_genes[1],    span = 0.8 )

title: Canonical Tocky Analysis for Temporal Gradient on a Transcriptomic Manifold

title: Canonical Tocky Analysis for Temporal Gradient on a Transcriptomic Manifold
text: Experimentally resolving temporal transcriptional dynamics single-cell resolution vivo major challenge, standard scRNA-seq provides cross-sectional “snapshots”. CanonicalTockySeq bridges gap integrating molecular clock T-cell receptor (TCR) signalling—based Nr4a3-Tocky Fluorescent Timer—scRNA-seq establish experimentally anchored temporal reference. using landmark Tocky fractions (New, Persistent, Arrested) biological ground truths, package constructs transcriptomic manifold canonical space unique conical geometry. allows decoupling temporal progression (geodesic angle) signalling strength (radial intensity).

title: Canonical Tocky Analysis for Temporal Gradient on a Transcriptomic Manifold
text: CanonicalTockySeq framework provides robust methodology capturing continuous, endogenous temporal resolution anchored biological history:

title: Canonical Tocky Analysis for Temporal Gradient on a Transcriptomic Manifold
text: Implements supervised Canonical Redundancy Analysis (RDA) designed single-cell Tocky data. Constrains high-dimensional transcriptomic space using reference Tocky signatures derive model-driven path differentiation. Projects individual single cells onto structure visualize state preserving biological heterogeneity.

title: Canonical Tocky Analysis for Temporal Gradient on a Transcriptomic Manifold
text: Utilizes Piecewise Spherical Linear Interpolation (SLERP) connect landmark biplot vectors (New → Persistent → Arrested). Generates curved, geodesic path along transcriptomic hypersphere, preventing artifacts inherent standard polynomial trajectories. Maps cells 0–90° Tocky Time coordinate (angle) normalized Tocky Intensity (magnitude).

title: Canonical Tocky Analysis for Temporal Gradient on a Transcriptomic Manifold
text: Orders genes peak activation timing reveal sequential transcriptional programs. Facilitates identification immediate-early committers, intermediate effectors, terminal exhaustion markers.

title: Canonical Tocky Analysis for Temporal Gradient on a Transcriptomic Manifold
text: Masahiro Ono Lab (MonoTockyLab) develops experimental computational approaches study immune cell dynamics, particular focus temporal regulation gene expression T cells. lab known development Tocky (Timer cell kinetics activity), platform uses Fluorescent Timer proteins analyse transcriptional signalling dynamics vivo single-cell resolution. research integrates mouse genetics, immunology, flow cytometry, single-cell omics, computational modelling. Current research directions include: cancer immunology immunotherapy temporal mechanisms T cell activation, differentiation, tolerance Foxp3 transcriptional dynamics regulation vivo computational methods time-resolved single-cell analysis, including CanonicalTockySeq Principal Investigator: Dr Masahiro Ono, Reader Immunology Imperial College London. Dr Ono creator Tocky, spanning transgenic reporter systems associated analytical frameworks.

what: Canonical Redundancy Analysis for Tocky Differentiation using single-cell RNA-sequencing data (CanonicalTockySeq) — CanonicalTockySeq
title: Canonical Redundancy Analysis for Tocky Differentiation using single-cell RNA-sequencing data (CanonicalTockySeq) — CanonicalTockySeq
text: Performs specialized Canonical Redundancy Analysis (RDA) designed single-cell Tocky data. architecture, genes treated "sites" cells "species", constrained Tocky-defined developmental stages (Blue, Blue-Red, Red).

title: Canonical Redundancy Analysis for Tocky Differentiation using single-cell RNA-sequencing data (CanonicalTockySeq) — CanonicalTockySeq
code: CanonicalTockySeq(X, Z)

title: Canonical Redundancy Analysis for Tocky Differentiation using single-cell RNA-sequencing data (CanonicalTockySeq) — CanonicalTockySeq
text: X Numeric matrix gene expression (Genes x Cells). Z Numeric matrix explanatory variables (Genes x Signatures). Typically contains Tocky-specific gene signatures (e.g., Blue, Blue-Red, Red).

title: Canonical Redundancy Analysis for Tocky Differentiation using single-cell RNA-sequencing data (CanonicalTockySeq) — CanonicalTockySeq
text: list containing: expression_scores Data frame Gene loadings (Genes x k). fitted_cell_scores Data frame Fitted Cell scores (Cells x k). cell_scores Data frame Cell scores (Cells x k). Use downstream GradientTockySeq. biplot Matrix biplot vectors constraint variables (Z).

title: Canonical Redundancy Analysis for Tocky Differentiation using single-cell RNA-sequencing data (CanonicalTockySeq) — CanonicalTockySeq
text: Implements two-step RDA procedure described Legendre & Legendre (2012): Multivariate regression Expression (X) Constraints (Z). PCA fitted values derive canonical axes.

title: Gradient Tocky Sequence (Slerp Model) — GradientTockySeq
text: Projects cells onto piecewise Slerp manifold defined landmarks B -> BR -> R. Cells negative correlations three landmarks classified "Timer Negative" (NA), lie ordination space opposite Tocky trajectory.

title: Gradient Tocky Sequence (Slerp Model) — GradientTockySeq
code: GradientTockySeq(res, B, BR, R, filter_negative = TRUE)

title: Gradient Tocky Sequence (Slerp Model) — GradientTockySeq
text: res List object returned CanonicalTockySeq (must contain $cell_scores coordinates). B Vector 'New' (Blue) landmark. BR Vector 'Persistent' (Blue-Red) landmark. R Vector 'Arrested' (Red) landmark. filter_negative Logical. TRUE, assigns NA cells negative projection three landmarks.

title: Gradient Tocky Sequence (Slerp Model) — GradientTockySeq
text: data frame containing: angle Tocky Time (0-90) intensity Raw Euclidean norm (distance origin) norm_intensity Intensity normalized Slerp trajectory similarity Cosine similarity manifold

what: Plot Pseudotime Heatmap (Ordered by Peak Timing) — PlotTockyHeatmap

text: Visualizes gene expression along Tocky trajectory. Supports ordering genes "Peak Time" (Cascade) Hierarchical Clustering.

title: Plot Pseudotime Heatmap (Ordered by Peak Timing) — PlotTockyHeatmap
code: PlotTockyHeatmap(   object,   gradient_res,   genes,   n_bins = 100,   ordering_method = c("peak", "cluster"),   span = 0.5,   scale_rows = TRUE )

title: Plot Pseudotime Heatmap (Ordered by Peak Timing) — PlotTockyHeatmap
text: object Seurat object expression matrix. gradient_res Data frame GradientTocky named vector angles. genes Character vector genes plot. n_bins Integer. Number bins (default 100). ordering_method Character. "peak" (sort max expression time) "cluster" (hclust). span Numeric. Smoothing span (default 0.5). scale_rows Logical. Z-score rows (default TRUE).

what: Run TradeSeq Differential Dynamics Analysis on Tocky Trajectory — RunTradeSeqTocky
title: Run TradeSeq Differential Dynamics Analysis on Tocky Trajectory — RunTradeSeqTocky
text: wrapper function perform differential dynamic expression testing using tradeSeq. Supports Seurat v5+ (uses 'layer') matrix inputs.

title: Run TradeSeq Differential Dynamics Analysis on Tocky Trajectory — RunTradeSeqTocky
code: RunTradeSeqTocky(   object,   gradient_res,   group_by,   genes,   n_knots = 5,   n_cores = 1 )

title: Run TradeSeq Differential Dynamics Analysis on Tocky Trajectory — RunTradeSeqTocky
text: object Seurat object sparse expression matrix (Genes x Cells). gradient_res data frame returned GradientTocky_Slerp (must contain $angle). group_by object Seurat: Character string metadata column name (e.g., "treatment"). object Matrix: named factor/character vector groups matching column names. genes Character vector genes test (e.g., output SelectTockyGenes). n_knots Integer. Number knots GAM (default 5). n_cores Integer. Number cores parallel processing (default 1).

title: Run TradeSeq Differential Dynamics Analysis on Tocky Trajectory — RunTradeSeqTocky
text: list containing: results Data frame differential dynamics results (p-values, FDR). gam_fit raw tradeSeq GAM object (downstream plotting).

Can named (c("WT"="black")) unnamed. span Numeric. Smoothing span LOESS (default 0.8). jitter_amount Numeric. Amount vertical jitter points (default 0.2). pt_alpha Numeric. Transparency single cell points (0-1). m Numeric. Magnification factor y-max.

title: CanonicalTockySeq 0.1.0
text: Initial Public Release: Beta release CanonicalTockySeq framework single-cell transcriptomic manifold analysis. GradientTockySeq: Implementation piecewise Spherical Linear Interpolation (SLERP) geodesic trajectory reconstruction. Conical Geometry: Introduction “Colimaçon” (Nautilus) coordinate system decouple temporal progression signaling intensity. Tocky Integration: Full support Nr4a3-Tocky Fluorescent Timer anchored landmark populations (New, Persistent, Arrested) tumour context.
